The piling of academic knowledge is fraught with the danger of vulgarity of speech - self-conceit, asī bōlavigār vigārah bōl. But merciful is Creation which continues to grace us, tū nadarī andar tōlah tōl.Real knowledge consists in attuning to the innate self-awareness that attunes us to the environment (universe) we are in, jah karanī tah pūrī mat. In Gurbani, solving the mystery of the Self comes through the grace of Guru-wisdom.

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਜਪ ਅਸੰਖ ਭਾਉ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh jap asankh bhāu .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless - the recitations,  Countless - the expressions of faith
scriptures.ss_pa: (ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਦੀ ਰਚਨਾ ਵਿਚ) ਅਨਗਿਣਤ ਜੀਵ ਜਪ ਕਰਦੇ ਹਨ, ਬੇਅੰਤ ਜੀਵ (ਹੋਰਨਾਂ ਨਾਲ) ਪਿਆਰ (ਦਾ ਵਰਤਾਉ) ਕਰ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਪੂਜਾ ਅਸੰਖ ਤਪ ਤਾਉ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh pūjā asankh tap tāu .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless – the modes of worship,  Countless – the practice of austerities
scriptures.ss_pa: ਕਈ ਜੀਵ ਪੂਜਾ ਕਰ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ ਅਤੇ ਅਨਗਿਣਤ ਹੀ ਜੀਵ ਤਪ ਸਾਧ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਗਰੰਥ ਮੁਖਿ ਵੇਦ ਪਾਠ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh garanth mukh vēd pāth .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless scriptures,  Countless readings.
scriptures.ss_pa: ਬੇਅੰਤ ਜੀਵ ਵੇਦਾਂ ਅਤੇ ਹੋਰ ਧਾਰਮਿਕ ਪੁਸਤਕਾਂ ਦੇ ਪਾਠ ਮੂੰਹ ਨਾਲ ਕਰ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਜੋਗ ਮਨਿ ਰਹਹਿ ਉਦਾਸ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh jōg man rahah udās .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless – the ways of seeking union (jog),  Countless renunciate.
scriptures.ss_pa: ਜੋਗ ਦੇ ਸਾਧਨ ਕਰਨ ਵਾਲੇ ਬੇਅੰਤ ਮਨੁੱਖ ਆਪਣੇ ਮਨ ਵਿਚ (ਮਾਇਆ ਵਲੋਂ) ਉਪਰਾਮ ਰਹਿੰਦੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਭਗਤ ਗੁਣ ਗਿਆਨ ਵੀਚਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh bhagat gun giān vīchār .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless devotees,  Countless their the excellences, knowledge, and wisdom
scriptures.ss_pa: (ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਦੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਵਿਚ) ਅਣਗਿਣਤ ਭਗਤ ਹਨ, ਜੋ ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਦੇ ਗੁਣਾਂ ਅਤੇ ਗਿਆਨ ਦੀ ਵਿਚਾਰ ਕਰ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ,

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਸਤੀ ਅਸੰਖ ਦਾਤਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh satī asankh dātār .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless Truth-seekers  Countless givers of charity
scriptures.ss_pa: ਅਨੇਕਾਂ ਹੀ ਦਾਨੀ ਤੇ ਦਾਤੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਸੂਰ ਮੁਹ ਭਖ ਸਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh sūr muh bhakh sār .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless the warriors of the spirit  Who face steely disciplines with courage
scriptures.ss_pa: (ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ ਦੀ ਰਚਨਾ ਵਿਚ) ਬੇਅੰਤ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਹਨ ਜੋ ਆਪਣੇ ਮੂੰਹਾਂ ਉੱਤੇ (ਭਾਵ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਹੋ ਕੇ) ਸ਼ਾਸਤ੍ਰਾਂ ਦੇ ਵਾਰ ਸਹਿੰਦੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਮੋਨਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ਤਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: asankh mōn liv lāi tār .
scriptures.kg_en: Countless practice silence,  To attune their minds.
scriptures.ss_pa: ਅਨੇਕਾਂ ਮੋਨੀ ਹਨ, ਜੋ ਇਕ-ਰਸ ਬ੍ਰਿਤੀ ਜੋੜ ਕੇ ਬੈਠ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਕਵਣ ਕਹਾ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
scriptures.roman: kudarat kavan kahā vīchār .
scriptures.kg_en: How can Your creation be described?
scriptures.ss_pa: ਮੇਰੀ ਕੀਹ ਤਾਕਤ ਹੈ ਕਿ ਕਰਤਾਰ ਦੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਦੀ ਵਿਚਾਰ ਕਰ ਸਕਾਂ।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਵਾਰਿਆ ਨ ਜਾਵਾ ਏਕ ਵਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: vāriā n jāvā ēk vār .
scriptures.kg_en: I am awe-struck, unworthy even to try!
scriptures.ss_pa: (ਹੇ ਅਕਾਲ ਪੁਰਖ!) ਮੈਂ ਤਾਂ ਤੇਰੇ ਉੱਤੋਂ ਇਕ ਵਾਰੀ ਭੀ ਸਦਕੇ ਹੋਣ ਜੋਗਾ ਨਹੀਂ ਹਾਂ (ਭਾਵ, ਮੇਰੀ ਹਸਤੀ ਬਹੁਤ ਹੀ ਤੁੱਛ ਹੈ।)

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਾਈ ਭਲੀ ਕਾਰ ॥
scriptures.roman: jō tudh bhāvai sāī bhalī kār .
scriptures.kg_en: Your Pleasure (Hukam)  Is my command.
scriptures.ss_pa: ਜੋ ਤੈਨੂੰ ਚੰਗਾ ਲਗਦਾ ਹੈ ਉਹੀ ਕੰਮ ਭਲਾ ਹੈ (ਭਾਵ, ਤੇਰੀ ਰਜ਼ਾ ਵਿਚ ਰਹਿਣਾ ਹੀ ਠੀਕ ਹੈ)।

scriptures.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਮਤਿ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ॥੧੭॥
scriptures.roman: tū sadā salāmat nirankār .17.
scriptures.kg_en: You: the Eternal, the Living, the Formless.
scriptures.ss_pa: ਹੇ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ! ਤੂੰ ਸਦਾ ਅਟੱਲ ਰਹਿਣ ਵਾਲਾ ਹੈਂ ॥੧੭॥

3_18.commentary_en: TRANSLATIONasankh jap asankh bhāuCountless - the recitations,Countless - the expressions of faithThe term Jup here refers to the practice of reciting mantras as opposed to the name of the text or baaṇee under consideration. Jup, as used here, was – and remains – a prevalent religious practice across faith traditions. Similarly, bhau in this context is an expression of devotion or longing. Guru Nanak speaks from the perspective of a panch: despite the innumerable methods, practices and profession of faiths, the true path is one of divine knowledge that is obtained from the true guru, vin satigur paratīt n āvaī nām n lāgō bhāu/ 65.asankh pūjā asankh tap tāu Countless – the modes of worship,Countless – the practice of austeritiesPūjā, like Jup, is the prevalent mode of religion and means the giving up, or offering something to incur favor with a deity. Tap tāu means to heat up and refers to the practices aimed at heating the body or producing inner heat in the expectation that it would purify the body and prepare it for the goal of moksha or liberation.True offering, however, is within, namely the exchange of haumai for hukam which comes from inscribing the sandalwood-like divine knowledge on the slate of the mind, tērā nām karī chananāthīā jē man urasā hōi /karanī kungū jē ralai ghat antar pūjā hōi /489.asankh garanth mukh vēd pāthCountless scriptures,Countless readings. Granth: an arrangement of words, a religious text, or a treatise that owes its authorship to the head of a religious sect and is read out loud, as in a chant. The true scripture is the one that flows from the Guru’s mouth, guramukh nādan guramukh vēdan guramukh rahiā samāī/2asankh jōg man rahah udāsCountless – the ways of seeking union (jog),Countless renunciates.Jōg is a variation on the word Yoga, which means to yoke or fasten to. Yogis yoked themselves to a particular practice or discipline to achieve the state of jog, union. So the word signifies both the training and the goal. This practice is often accompanied by withdrawal (udās), although it also refers to a sect of yogis called udāsis, attributed to Sri Chand, Guru Nanak’s son.asankh bhagat gun giān vīchār.Countless devotees,Countless their the excellences, knowledge, and wisdom Bhagat: one who is gifted, fortunate, refers to devotees who contemplate divine qualities, experience, and insight.asankh satī asankh dātārCountless Truth-seekersCountless givers of charity  Satī: a virtuous woman, here used as an appellation for Truth-seekers who conceive of themselves as brides of the divine. Dātār: those engaged in charitable giving as a religious activity.But, the truly virtuous are receive their instruction from the Guru within kah nānak gur nām drirhāiā har har nām har satī/573asankh sūr muh bhakh sār.Countless the warriors of the spiritWho face steely disciplines with courage asankh mōn liv lāi tār .Countless practice silence,To attune their minds.  But without intuitive knowledge all these practices cannot overcome the obstacle of haumai - nām n japaī kiu sukh pāvai bin nāvai kiu sōhai/mūnd mudāi jatā sikh bādhī mōn rahai abhimānākudarat kavan kahā vīchār .How can Your creation be described?  vāriā n jāvā ēk vār .I am awe-struck, unworthy even to try! jō tudh bhāvai sāī bhalī kār .Your Pleasure (Hukam)Is my command.   tū sadā salāmat nirankār  You: the Eternal, the Living, the Formless.      INTERPRETATIONThe concluding lines of paurees 16 -19  share a familiar refrain: jō tudh bhāvai sāī bhalī kār. This is a pattern in Jup baaṇee where Guru Nanak clusters passages together by threading them with a shared concluding catchphrase. In Paurees 16-19, the underlying message  as always, to hearken us back to hukam. In these passages, this message is played back in different contexts. In this context, the panch's role discussed in pauree 16 comes into play because the panch is the very embodiment of hukam.  Pauree 17 contrasts the spectrum of religious and spiritual practice in vogue with the way of Gurmat with the enlightened consciousness of the panch as a backdrop. At first glance, the seventeenth pauree appears to be a litany of various spiritual practices. But if we examine the pauree closely, Guru Nanak is not simply cataloguing the various methods, nor is he putting his stamp of approval on them. Quite the contrary.Recall that beginning with pauree 1, and onwards, Guruji has repeatedly called out these practices as ineffective and misdirected. A panch, as we saw in pauree 16, affirms that the voice of hukam, that speaks in the recesses of the consciousness, is the true command that must be obeyed.  What that means is that our actions must be in harmony with and purposeful in advancing the larger creative process.Each one of these activities is addressed individually in Gurbani, and their real import, from a Gurmat perspective, is explained. It is a warning, yet again, not to confuse ideologies, ritual, scriptural readings and charitable giving for the real thing: surrender or submission to hukam, a path that does not invite resistance or counter reactions.
